_doc: NOLAN composition archetype registry — the SHARED layout vocabulary every theme references and both the block composer AND the bespoke agent read (one dialect). A scene's archetype = its macro layout. Themes declare composition:{default,allowed[]} pointing at these ids; the bespoke agent picks from a theme's allowed set given the beat; blocks are classified by archetype. Design: docs/COMPOSITION_ARCHITECTURE.md. Vocabulary grounded in the 26 themes + 18 blocks (editorial/presentation/broadcast), with the composable-primitive discipline of 'Every Layout'. Anchors are GRID guidance (rule-of-thirds / 12-column), not pixels — robust across content length, font width, aspect (like data-fit for text). Exemplars are the biggest LLM-quality lever: seeded by promoting real renders + hand-authoring gaps (see exemplar field; null = not yet promoted).

grid._doc: The substrate ALL archetypes place against — not an archetype itself. A 1920x1080 frame divided into 12 columns and rule-of-thirds horizontal bands. Anchors below reference these.

safe_areas.caption_keep_out: content stays in the TOP ~85% (bottom ~15% is where the caption bar sits)
safe_areas.title_safe: keep primary content inside a ~5% inset from every edge (advisory)

grid._machine_doc: The numeric fields (caption_keep_out_y, title_safe_inset, per-archetype `zone`) are the machine-readable contract the deterministic layout linter (nolan.hyperframes.layout_lint) checks a composed frame's declared geometry against. Coords are 0..1 fractions of the 1920x1080 canvas (x→right, y→down). caption_keep_out_y=0.85 is calibrated to where the composer's caption bar actually sits (bottom ~9-15cqh); a content box whose BOTTOM dips below it is a HARD error. The HARD out-of-bounds check flags only genuinely OFF-CANVAS content (coords <0 or >1); the title_safe_inset is a nicety the composer already roughly respects, so it is NOT gated. `zone` is the box a scene's content-centroid should fall inside — an ADVISORY anchor check (fuzzy, area-weighted).

centered-hero.intent: One dominant idea, dead-centre, using the full canvas.
centered-hero.when_to_use: the beat has ONE thing to say — a single statement, a big number, a big question, a thesis, a reveal.
centered-hero.not_for: lists, comparisons, or dense multi-part data.
centered-hero.anchor: primary element at optical centre; a short eyebrow on the upper third, optional support on the lower third. Symmetric about the vertical axis.

editorial-column.intent: Text set as a reading column against a margin axis, ragged right — the Vox/editorial voice.
editorial-column.when_to_use: narration-led running claims, a quote with attribution, a labelled statement — text carries the beat.
editorial-column.not_for: a single hero word (use centered-hero) or media-forward beats.
editorial-column.anchor: column occupies the left ~55% (columns 1-7) with a rule/margin at its edge; content top-aligned to the upper third and flowing down.

swiss-grid.intent: A modular grid — several items placed on columns and rows with consistent gutters.
swiss-grid.when_to_use: an enumeration, a multi-item structure, a small matrix, several stats or images together.
swiss-grid.not_for: a single idea (use centered-hero) or a two-way comparison (use split-screen).
swiss-grid.anchor: items laid on the 12-column grid with even gutters and a shared baseline; a strong top rule or header row.

split-screen.intent: Two zones side by side, in dialogue.
split-screen.when_to_use: a comparison, before/after, this-vs-that, a two-sided argument (VISUAL via comparison, TEXTUAL/numeric via juxtaposition).
split-screen.not_for: a single idea, or more than two items (use swiss-grid).
split-screen.anchor: left half (cols 1-6) + right half (cols 7-12) with a seam at centre; each half's content centred within its zone; an optional 'vs' hinge on the seam.

full-bleed-overlay.intent: Media or atmosphere fills the frame edge-to-edge; text is overlaid on a legible zone.
full-bleed-overlay.when_to_use: a photo/video/map/gradient IS the hero; an establishing or atmospheric beat.
full-bleed-overlay.not_for: type-only beats (nothing to fill the bleed).
full-bleed-overlay.anchor: media on track 0 edge-to-edge; a scrim (track 1) for contrast; text confined to a safe zone (lower third, or a corner panel) — never floating over busy media.

focal-card.intent: One object or subject anchors the stage; supporting text orbits it.
focal-card.when_to_use: a background-removed subject, a hero object, a news card, a document, a portrait.
focal-card.not_for: beats with no single focal object.
focal-card.anchor: focal element centred (or offset to a thirds line); label/kicker flanking it or on the lower third; the focal object owns the middle band.

sidebar.intent: A narrow fixed rail beside a wide flexible content zone.
sidebar.when_to_use: an index/number/label/step marker beside the main content; a running marker + body.
sidebar.anchor: a narrow rail (~cols 1-3: a number, label, or index) + a wide content zone (cols 4-12) filling the rest; the rail is a persistent marker, the body carries the beat.

framed.intent: A self-contained artefact presented cleanly within a margin frame.
framed.when_to_use: a chart, a code block, a pull-quote, a diagram — an object to present, not narrate.
framed.anchor: artefact centred within a generous margin (frame inset ~10-14%); a caption or source on the lower third; the frame itself may carry the theme's rule/decoration.

timeline.intent: A sequence of events or steps connected along a spine, with directional flow.
timeline.when_to_use: the beat walks a SEQUENCE — a chronology, a history, a process, milestones, 'then… then… then', how-it-works steps.
timeline.not_for: unordered items (use swiss-grid), a two-way comparison (use split-screen), or a single moment (use centered-hero).
timeline.anchor: a spine (horizontal left→right or vertical top→bottom) with a node at each event; year/label callouts sit off the spine; draw order / arrows carry the directional flow.

comparison-table.intent: A tabular matrix — options across the top, criteria down the side, cells comparing them.
comparison-table.when_to_use: a structured many-way comparison: options x features, a spec table, a yes/partial/no matrix.
comparison-table.not_for: a two-way this-vs-that (use split-screen) or a uniform item grid (use swiss-grid).
comparison-table.anchor: a header row of column labels over a heavier rule, row-labels down the left, cells (chips/values) on the grid; one column may be highlighted as 'ours'.

asymmetric-hero.intent: A rule-of-thirds hero — the subject fills two thirds, the title breathes in the opposite negative space (the cinematic opening / chapter card).
asymmetric-hero.when_to_use: a filmic opening, a chapter/section card, a portrait or establishing shot where one subject and a short title share the frame asymmetrically.
asymmetric-hero.not_for: a symmetric centred idea (use centered-hero), a running reading column (editorial-column), a two-item contrast (split-screen), a data device.
asymmetric-hero.anchor: the subject occupies ~two thirds of the frame; the title sits in the lower third of the OPPOSITE side over a directional scrim; generous negative space between.

quadrant.intent: A two-axis positioning plot — two labelled dimensions cross into four quadrants; items sit by their (x,y) values.
quadrant.when_to_use: positioning things on TWO dimensions at once — effort vs impact, cost vs quality, risk vs reward, a 2x2 matrix, mapping trade-offs.
quadrant.not_for: a single ranking or list (use ledger/bullet_list), a time sequence (timeline), a tabular matrix of discrete options+criteria (comparison-table), a one-axis scale (stat).
quadrant.anchor: a centred plot box; two axes crossing at the middle (x horizontal, y vertical) with end labels; items placed by (x,y) in 0..1 (0 = left/bottom); optional quadrant corner labels.

ledger.intent: A dense hairline-separated row-list — ordinal, title, description, meta per row.
ledger.when_to_use: a table of contents, an index, an agenda, a catalogue, a chaptered list of items.
ledger.not_for: a short bulleted list (use bullet_list / editorial-column) or 2D item cards (use swiss-grid).
ledger.anchor: rows stacked full-width, each a numeral + title + description + meta, separated by hairlines under a heavier top rule.
